chunks.text: Venkat Anantharam is a Professor in EECS at UC Berkeley. Contact information: 271 Cory Hall, tel: 510-643-8435, email: ananth@eecs.berkeley.edu. Office hours vary by week; email for appointment. Research support contact: Stephanie Bratton, sbratton@berkeley.edu.

Biography: He received his B.Tech in Electrical Engineering from IIT in 1980, M.S. in EE from UC Berkeley in 1982, M.A. in Mathematics from UC Berkeley in 1983, C.Phil in Mathematics from UC Berkeley in 1984, and Ph.D. in EE from UC Berkeley in 1986. Prior to joining UC Berkeley faculty in 1994, he was at Cornell University.

Research areas: Information, Data, Network, and Communication Sciences (IDNCS), Artificial Intelligence (AI), Control, Intelligent Systems, and Robotics (CIR), Security (SEC), Signal Processing (SP). Research centers: Berkeley Laboratory for Information and System Sciences (BLISS), Center for the Theoretical Foundations of Learning, Inference, Information, Intelligence, Mathematics and Microeconomics at Berkeley (CLIMB).
Teaching schedule: EE 223 Stochastic Systems: Estimation and Control, MoWe 17:00-18:29, Cory 293. EE 227BT Convex Optimization, MoWe 15:30-16:59, Cory 540AB.

Selected publications include works on Reed-Muller codes, priority queue emulation, multiterminal source-coding, Kikuchi approximation, regulating functions, Lyapunov exponents, timing channel reliability, congestion games, generalized distributive law, interference avoidance, CDMA sequences, Internet rate control, LDPC decoders, VLSI architectures, water-filling in channels, input-queued switch throughput, common randomness capacity, bits through queues, and adaptive allocation schemes.
Selected honors and awards: IEEE Information Theory Society Paper Award (2023, 1998), IEEE ComSoc Stephen O. Rice Prize (2000), IEEE Fellow (1998), NSF Presidential Young Investigator (1988).
